description_long: This object is a heavily weathered and partially destroyed airplane fuselage, featuring a row of jagged, broken windows and exposed internal structures. The exterior is predominantly a faded, light gray with patches of rust and debris, suggesting long-term abandonment or crash damage. The wings are missing or severely damaged, with only the stubs and some protruding metallic elements remaining along the lower sides. The fuselage is lined with a series of open doorways and window frames, some of which are bent or warped, and the undercarriage is littered with remnants of landing gear and structural supports. The tail section is absent, leaving the rear end open and exposing the internal framework. Various colored panels and remnants of paint are visible, adding to the impression of a derelict, urban crash site.
description: A derelict, broken airplane fuselage with missing wings and tail, featuring exposed interiors, jagged windows, and weathered, rusted surfaces.
description_view_0: Side view showing the narrow profile, with jagged protrusions and broken window frames along the length.
description_view_1: Top-down view revealing the open, hollow fuselage and the arrangement of doorways and window openings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, highlighting the damaged lower edge and more visible internal structure.
description_view_3: Bottom or underside view displaying the row of landing gear stubs and the full extent of the fuselage's damage and openings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight, broken stubs and no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: No engines visible; nacelle mounts or engine housings are absent or destroyed.
attribute_tail_design: Tail section missing; no vertical or horizontal stabilizers present.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed landing gear remnants with multiple main gear stubs visible along the underside.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple window frames; canopy and glass are missing or broken.
